falsehood; literally (untruth) or figuratively (idol)
fraud
to loathe, i.e. (morally) detest

tokens.range: to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in
properly, to entwine as ascreen; by implication, to fence in, cover over, (figuratively) protect

properly, to creak (or emit a stridulous sound), i.e. to shout (usually for joy)
an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character
probably to brighten up, i.e. (figuratively) be (causatively, make) blithe or gleesome

flags.note: עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

an eye (literally or figuratively); by analogy, a fountain (as the eye of the landscape)

something said

1.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.text: For thou art not a God
tokens.range: strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
properly, to turn aside from the road (for a lodging or any other purpose), i.e. sojourn (as a guest); also to shrink, fear (as in a strange place); also to gather forhostility (as afraid)
pleased with
bad or (as noun) evil (natural or moral)
a wrong (especially moral)

gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
a king

tokens.text: for unto thee will I pray
tokens.range: to judge (officially or mentally); by extension, to intercede, pray
a voice or sound
to prick up the ears, i.e. hearken
a halloo

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.

to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)

tokens.text: But as for me I will come
tokens.range: to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
a large public building, such as a palace or temple
kindness; by implication (towards God) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty
fear (also used as infinitive); morally, reverence
a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity
abundance (in any respect)
to depress, i.e. prostrate (especially reflexive, in homage to royalty or God)

flags.note: חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.

צְדָקָה — "righteousness" (moral quality) vs. "justice" (social-legal action). OT prophets use this for God's vindicating action for the oppressed; "righteousness" alone misses the social dimension.
